subtopics.title: What's in the Box
subtopics.content:
#### Per Hero Set
- **1** hero figure (detailed miniature)
- **1** sidekick figure (if the hero has one)
- **1** hero card — shows health, special ability, and sidekick info
- **30-card deck** — unique to that hero, with attack cards, defense cards, versatile cards, and scheme cards
- **1** health dial or health tracker

#### Shared Components
- **1** game board (double-sided map with different layouts)
- **Map tiles** — zones connected by lines showing movement paths
- **Combat tokens** — optional, for tracking effects

#### Available Hero Sets (varies by box)
- Battle of Legends Vol. 1: Medusa, King Arthur, Alice, Sinbad
- Battle of Legends Vol. 2: Achilles, Bloody Mary, Sun Wukong, Yennenga
- Cobble & Fog: Sherlock Holmes, Dracula, Invisible Man, Jekyll & Hyde
- Jurassic Park: Raptors, T. Rex, Dr. Sattler, Muldoon
- Marvel sets, Buffy sets, and more

Note: Any hero from any set can fight any hero from any other set. The system is fully cross-compatible.

subtopics.title: Board Layout
subtopics.content:
1. **Choose a map** — Select one side of the game board. Each map has a different layout of zones and connections.
2. **Zones** — The map is divided into zones connected by lines. Heroes move along these connections.
3. **Ranged zones** — Some zones are marked as having line-of-sight to others, enabling ranged attacks.
4. **Starting positions** — Each player places their hero (and sidekick) on designated starting zones on opposite sides of the map.
5. **Separate zones** — Hero and sidekick can occupy the same zone or different zones.

subtopics.title: Player Setup
subtopics.content:
#### Each Player
- **Choose a hero** — Select any hero from any Unmatched set.
- **Take the hero's 30-card deck** — Shuffle it. This is your draw pile.
- **Place hero figure** — On your starting zone.
- **Place sidekick** — On the same starting zone (if your hero has one).
- **Set health** — Set your hero's health dial to the number shown on the hero card (typically 13-17 HP).
- **Set sidekick health** — If applicable (typically 1-8 HP).
- **Draw opening hand** — Draw **5 cards** from your deck.

#### 2-Player Setup
- Each player controls 1 hero.

#### 3-4 Player Setup
- Can be played as free-for-all or 2v2 teams.

subtopics.title: Starting the Game
subtopics.content:
- **5-card hand** — Each player starts with 5 cards drawn.
- **Starting player** — Determined randomly. Alternate turns.
- **No setup effects** — Heroes start with no active effects or tokens.
- **Objective** — Reduce your opponent's hero to 0 health.
- **Sidekick note** — Defeating an opponent's sidekick is useful but does NOT win the game. Only the hero matters.

subtopics.title: Turn Structure
subtopics.content:
On your turn, you take **2 actions** (can repeat the same action):

#### Action Options
1. **Maneuver** — Draw 1 card, then optionally move your hero and/or sidekick.
2. **Scheme** — Play a Scheme card from your hand for its effect.
3. **Attack** — Declare an attack against an opponent's fighter (hero or sidekick) in range.

#### Important Notes
- You can take the **same action twice** (e.g., Maneuver + Maneuver, or Attack + Attack).
- Drawing cards via Maneuver is the only way to replenish your hand.
- If your deck runs out, you do NOT reshuffle — you simply cannot draw. Running out of cards is dangerous.

subtopics.title: Actions & Options
subtopics.content:
#### Maneuver
- Draw **1 card** from your deck.
- Then you may move your hero up to their **movement value** (number of zone connections).
- You may also move your sidekick up to their movement value.
- Movement is optional — you can draw without moving.

#### Scheme
- Play a card with the **Scheme** type from your hand.
- Resolve its text effect (draw extra cards, deal damage, place tokens, reposition, etc.).
- Discard the card after use.

#### Attack (Combat)
1. **Declare target** — Choose an opponent's fighter in your attack range (melee = same zone, ranged = zones with line of sight).
2. **Play attack card** — Place an attack card from your hand face-down.
3. **Defender plays defense card** — The defending player places a defense card face-down (or chooses not to defend).
4. **Reveal simultaneously** — Flip both cards.
5. **Resolve effects** — Resolve any "During Combat" effects on either card.
6. **Calculate damage** — Attack value minus defense value = damage dealt to the defender. Minimum 0.
7. **After Combat effects** — Resolve any "After Combat" text.
8. **Discard both cards**.

#### Card Types
- **Attack cards** — Have an attack value. Can only be played when attacking.
- **Defense cards** — Have a defense value. Can only be played when defending.
- **Versatile cards** — Have BOTH attack and defense values. Can be used for either.
- **Scheme cards** — Played as the Scheme action. Cannot be used in combat.

subtopics.title: Special Mechanics
subtopics.content:
#### Asymmetric Decks
- Each hero's 30-card deck is **completely unique**.
- Decks reflect the hero's fighting style: Medusa has gaze attacks, King Arthur has noble defense, Alice grows/shrinks for different stats.
- Learning your deck's strengths and weaknesses is key.

#### Sidekicks
- Most heroes have a sidekick (e.g., King Arthur has Merlin, Medusa has Harpies).
- Sidekicks can move, attack, and be attacked independently.
- Some sidekick attacks use the hero's cards; others have unique mechanics.
- Defeating a sidekick does NOT win the game but removes a threat.

#### Boost
- Some cards have a **Boost** ability: if you draw this card during the Maneuver action, you may immediately reveal it for a bonus effect instead of adding it to your hand.

#### Deck Exhaustion
- Your deck does NOT reshuffle. When it's empty, you cannot draw.
- An aggressive player who plays cards quickly may run out of options.
- Managing hand size and card economy is critical.

#### Range
- **Melee fighters** — Must be in the same zone to attack.
- **Ranged fighters** — Can attack targets in zones connected by line-of-sight.
- Some heroes are melee, some are ranged, and some have both options.

subtopics.title: Endgame & Scoring
subtopics.content:
#### Win Condition
The game ends immediately when a **hero is reduced to 0 health**. The player who dealt the killing blow wins.

#### In Multi-Player Games
- **Free-for-all** — Last hero standing wins.
- **Team (2v2)** — A team wins when both opposing heroes are defeated.

#### No Points
There is no scoring system. The game is purely win/lose based on elimination.

#### Match Length
Games are fast (20-40 minutes), encouraging best-of-3 matches or switching heroes.

subtopics.title: Opening Priorities
subtopics.content:
- **Maneuver first** — Your opening turns should usually be Maneuver actions to build hand size and position.
- **Learn your deck** — Know what cards are in your 30-card deck. If you know your best attack is coming, save defense for later.
- **Position matters** — Get into favorable zones before attacking. Ranged heroes want distance; melee heroes want adjacency.
- **Protect your sidekick** — If your sidekick is fragile, keep them behind your hero.

subtopics.title: Common Mistakes
subtopics.content:
- **Attacking without cards** — If your hand is empty, you can't play attack or defense cards. Never be caught with 0 cards.
- **Wasting defense on weak attacks** — If an opponent plays a weak attack (value 2), sometimes taking the damage is better than using a strong defense card.
- **Ignoring card economy** — Your deck is finite. Every card you play is gone forever. Play efficiently.
- **Chasing the sidekick** — Killing the sidekick doesn't win. Focus on the hero unless the sidekick is a major threat.
- **Predictable patterns** — Vary your actions. If you always attack, your opponent will always save defense.

subtopics.title: Key Decisions
subtopics.content:
- **Attack or Maneuver** — Attacking is tempting but costs cards. Maneuvering restocks your hand.
- **Which card to play in combat** — Save your strongest attacks for when the opponent is low on defense cards.
- **Defend or take damage** — Sometimes absorbing a hit to save a defense card for a bigger attack is correct.
- **Target hero or sidekick** — Sidekicks are easier to kill but don't win the game.
- **When to commit** — At some point you must go all-in on attacking. Timing this correctly wins games.
